text.title: terraform-state Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for terraform-state as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Local tfstate files in git repositories.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: State contains plaintext credentials and resource IDs. Once committed, secrets cannot be fully purged from git history.

failure-mode.description: Using terraform state push to overwrite remote state without a backup or plan verification.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Push overwrites the remote state immediately. If the pushed state is incorrect, there is no automatic rollback — you must recover from versioning.

failure-mode.description: Storing backend credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) as static CI secrets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Static keys never expire and are frequently leaked in CI logs. OIDC short-lived tokens are scoped to the specific workflow run and expire immediately after.
